key_questions: Severity: mild (comedones, <20 papules), moderate (20–100 papules, some pustules, nodules), severe (nodules >5, cysts, scarring)?
Current treatments tried (OTC retinoids, benzoyl peroxide, niacinamide)?
Female: relation to menstrual cycle? PCOS features?
Medications worsening acne: corticosteroids, lithium, iodides, androgens?
red_flags: Acne fulminans: sudden onset severe nodular acne + fever + arthralgia → urgent dermatology
Signs of PCOS in women: irregular menses, hirsutism → appropriate investigation
Psychological impact (anxiety, depression, social withdrawal) — address actively

first_line.name: Benzoyl Peroxide
first_line.notes: Most effective OTC acne treatment. Bactericidal (no resistance), comedolytic, anti-inflammatory. Start with 2.5% (less irritating than 5% or 10% — same efficacy). Apply to entire affected area (not just spots). Bleaches fabrics — warn patient. Takes 6–8 weeks for full benefit.
first_line.preferred_when: Mild inflammatory acne (papules/pustules); also prevents antibiotic resistance when combined with topical antibiotics

first_line.name: Topical Retinoid
first_line.drugs: Adapalene 0.1% gel (Differin — OTC) OD at bedtime | Tretinoin 0.025–0.05% cream (Retin-A — prescription)
first_line.notes: Normalize follicular keratinization, reduce comedones, anti-inflammatory. Adapalene 0.1% (Differin): OTC in Canada — excellent tolerability. Tretinoin: prescription, slightly more potent. Apply pea-sized amount to ENTIRE face (not spot treatment) at bedtime. Initial irritation (dryness, peeling) peaks weeks 2–4 then improves. Sun protection essential. Takes 12+ weeks for full benefit.
first_line.preferred_when: Comedonal acne (blackheads/whiteheads); moderate acne; long-term maintenance

second_line.name: Topical Antibiotic + BPO Combination
second_line.drugs: Clindamycin 1% + benzoyl peroxide 5% gel (Benzaclin, Clindoxyl) OD–BID
second_line.notes: Clindamycin 1% topical: anti-inflammatory, reduces P. acnes. ALWAYS combine with benzoyl peroxide to prevent antibiotic resistance. Never use topical antibiotic alone (without BPO) — resistance risk.

second_line.name: Combined OCP (females)
second_line.drugs: Ethinyl estradiol + drospirenone (Yaz, Yasmin) | EE + norgestimate (Tri-Cyclen)
second_line.notes: For women with acne ± PCOS — reduces androgenic stimulation of sebaceous glands. Health Canada approved for acne. Takes 3+ months for full benefit.

non_pharm: Gentle non-comedogenic cleanser BID (not scrubbing — worsens inflammation)
Non-comedogenic SPF 30+ sunscreen (especially with retinoid use)
Do not pick or squeeze (causes scarring, spreads bacteria)
Manage psychological impact — refer if significant mental health effect
treatment.refer_when: Moderate-severe acne unresponsive to topicals; nodular/cystic acne; scarring; consideration for oral isotretinoin (Accutane) — requires dermatologist; acne with significant psychological distress
